abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit layout engine.

inlineContent.text: The keyboard layout of a software keyboard can vary between locales and devices, which means that a layout engine must be flexible.
In KeyboardKit, a
is used to define the full set of keys of a keyboard. A layout can be created from an
, which defines only the input keys. This allows us to reuse identical layouts for many compatible input sets.
KeyboardKit defines some input sets like
, and
, as well as a standard keyboard layout that is compatible with
and similar input sets.
👑 KeyboardKit Pro unlocks more input sets, like
and
, and localized multi-device layouts for all supported locales. You can sign up for KeyboardKit Pro from the
KeyboardKit has a
type that defines a full keyboard layout. It’s also a namespace for other layout-related types, like
, which has a
function that returns a standard configuration for a certain context.
In KeyboardKit an
specifies the input keys of a keyboard. KeyboardKit comes with a few input sets, like
, and
, while KeyboardKit Pro unlocks many more, like
, etc.
A
can be generated from an alphabetic, numeric, and symbolic input set. Certain locales support multiple
s and can be added multiple times with different layouts, like
, etc.
The
will automatically apply the default layout for the current
, and many other parameters. You can inject a custom
if you for instance want to adjust certain keys.
KeyboardKit can generate a
layout for the current
. This makes it easy to adjust the standard layout, using layout extensions like
, etc.
KeyboardKit Pro unlocks more input sets, like
and
, as well as localized input sets and layouts for all
locales that are included in your license.
You can access localized layouts with the throwing builders. like
. These builders will throw a license error if you try to access them without a valid license. If you are on the Basic or Silver plan, you must specify which locales to use in your
KeyboardKit Pro extends
with even more functionality, to make it easier to add, remove, and replace items. See the
documentation and its nested
and
types for a full list of additional capabilities.
KeyboardKit Pro extends
with a list of
and makes the
add a layout picker to all locales that support multiple layouts.
This means languages like
can be added many times, with different layouts. The following locales support multiple layouts:
All localized layout implementations use the
to adjust their alphabetic input set for the current layout type.
